source_evidence.analysis_scope: Observed logos, editorial compositions, product renders, industrial controls, automotive interfaces and mobile UI references.
Realistic premium home-audio ecommerce flow across hero, specifications, controls, cart, checkout, responsive behavior and footer.

typography.guidance: Modern sans-serif typography and controlled contrast of scale and weight are recurrent; no universal family is selected and script remains accent-only when contextually justified.

color.guidance: An accent family may use controlled gradients when a project direction explicitly selects them.

composition.guidance: Minimalism must remain intentional and high information density needs perceptual control.

imagery_and_render.guidance: Premium product photography or synthetic rendering, clear subject separation and materially credible presentation are recurrent when a physical product is central.

materiality.guidance: Glassmorphism is controlled and localized, never a default surface treatment.

controls.guidance: Control affordance must be reviewed before direction approval; no component system is selected.

information_density.guidance: Dense information is acceptable only when hierarchy, contrast, grouping, and negative space remain perceptually controlled.

motion.guidance: Motion is subject to interaction-state and reduced-motion review; no aesthetic motion preference is inferred.

accessibility_boundaries.guidance: Contrast, reflow, zoom, state completeness, and reduced motion are mandatory review boundaries.

preferred.statement: Intentional minimalism and perceptually controlled information density are explicit constraints.
Immediate hierarchy and one dominant product or purpose are recurrent preference signals.
Specific editorial or geometric composition is preferred over generic template appearance.
Finished, comprehensible controls and complete visual states are preferred.
In premium physical-product ecommerce, clean composition, precise spacing, optical symmetry and defined iconography formed the strongest accepted base.
A substantive footer and controlled depth or motion are preferred over a flat, generic or abruptly ending composition when context supports them.
Controls must match their semantic role; descriptive captions must not appear as buttons, while social destinations may use icon-only controls with accessible names.

contextual.statement: Localized glassmorphism, script accents, controlled gradients, and product heroes depend on selected project direction.
A neutral base with focused saturated accent is recurrent but palette remains product-dependent.
Industrial or technical precision applies when supported by product context.
Warm Paper and Wine, editorial hierarchy, acoustic visualization and premium industrial rendering succeeded for AERA One but remain product-specific.

insufficient.statement: No universal font family, palette, icon system, material treatment or cross-product archetype is approved.

rejected.statement: Personal preference must not define global Symphonie identity or a fixed archetype aesthetic.
Reference images may not be copied into the repository or claimed as owned.

The Alpha.7 directions VD-01, VD-02, and VD-03 are rejected as visual baselines for the motorcycle pilot because their interface treatment lacked professionalism, finish, and product-context fidelity.
AERA One V4 dark high-tech redesign is rejected for this product direction because it was judged inferior to V3 and more generic; dark interfaces are not universally rejected.

reconciliations.rule: High information density requires perceptual control.
Accent family may include controlled gradients.
Personal preference does not define global Symphonie identity.

preference_model.application_rule: Resolve the product context and applicable evidence before choosing any contextual variable. The profile guides qualification and incompatibility detection; it never selects a universal style.
